records.ety: [OF. desconfiture, F. dconfiture. See Discomfort, v. t., and cf. Comfiture.]

defs: The act of discomfiting, or the state of being discomfited; rout; overthrow; defeat; frustration; confusion and dejection.
quotes: Every man's sword was against his fellow, and there was a very great discomfiture.
A hope destined to end . . . in discomfiture and disgrace.
